title: Anno Khara Expo Interview Eva Going Gundam Films TV etc From Different Directors Possible
display_title: Anno Khara Expo Interview Eva Going Gundam Films TV etc From Different Directors Possible
wikitext:
[]: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Fri Dec 02, 2016 10:57 pm Source. This was also on Anime News Network's newsfeed. There are a bunch of fascinating revelations in this booklet, if this is indeed accurate. It describes how Anno left Gainax because he felt he had run out of things to do and formed Khara primarily as an anime planning company or a think tank rather than an actual production company, but that changed once the 1.0 project started to gather steam. Here's the most important and game-changing excerpt: According to Anno, Evangelion will continue to be a mainstay of Khara’s output, and even identity. Anno’s overall attitude appears to be that his involvement in the animation industry is simply one of love of the craft–whether it be from the perspective of creator, archived materials curator, or businessman. As he himself states, it is his way of “giving back.” However, because he himself is an animation fan, he admits he can agree with the sentiment of fans who feel that he keeps going back to Eva for the money. And the way he justifies it is very blunt: making money is important, otherwise you cannot reinvest in something else to continue to create. For the future, he wishes Evangelion will become a cultural icon, one of the cornerstones of anime, much like Gundam, which has become an institution in and of itself. Like Gundam, where many creators can play around with the elements and create their own worlds, it is Anno’s hope that Evangelion can also be set free, to be reconstructed and reinterpreted in many ways by many other artists.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by cyharding » Sat Dec 03, 2016 12:10 am This raises a couple of questions for me. The first is whether any other anime director in the immediate future will want to touch the property considering the impact it had on the anime industry. The second is if Eva having multiple versions like the Gundam franchise would devalue the original in some way. Guy Nacks wrote: I just realized that is a terrible metaphor.

[]: The Future Of Evangelion             Quote Post by Apox » Sat Dec 03, 2016 12:15 am Could be interesting, I'm sure some diehards will dislike this, but I'm okay with more evangelion even tho it might not be done by anno.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Guy Nacks » Sat Dec 03, 2016 12:38 am cyharding wrote: This raises a couple of questions for me. The first is whether any other anime director in the immediate future will want to touch the property considering the impact it had on the anime industry. The second is if Eva having multiple versions like the Gundam franchise would devalue the original in some way.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Dec 09, 2016 4:06 pm ... I'm going to have to process this information a bit longer. I don't know what to feel about Evangelion being a franchise opened to that many different directors. I mean, I guess it's no different than what they've been doing with the many, many different manga versions of the property, but I'm still not sure that I like the concept.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Reichu » Fri Dec 09, 2016 5:44 pm @OP, I very distinctly remember hearing something to this effect before, though at this point it's hazy whether it was from an official statement somewhere or just fan speculah... I've been able to find an example of the latter through searches, but none of the former. Not terribly satisfying! Does anyone else feel like they heard about "Eva going Gundam" years ago?

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Ray » Fri Dec 09, 2016 6:58 pm Personally speaking I'm just glad Eva is going to have a Post Anno, post-rebuild life. Especially considering the obstacles the last rebuild movie has to surmount in order to be a worthy conclusion to Shinji's story. Maybe I'll get my Shinji ikari raising project anime adaptation?

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Fri Dec 09, 2016 7:01 pm I'm fine with it. Eva's been farmed out for years for video games, manga, novels and so on without Anno at the helm, and this really isn't any different. Might be good to have someone else's take on the property.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by FreakyFilmFan4ever » Fri Dec 09, 2016 9:39 pm Ray wrote: Personally speaking I'm just glad Eva is going to have a Post Anno, post-rebuild life. Especially considering the obstacles the last rebuild movie has to surmount in order to be a worthy conclusion to Shinji's story.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Dec 10, 2016 3:26 am Reichu wrote: Does anyone else feel like they heard about "Eva going Gundam" years ago?

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by hui43210 » Sat Dec 10, 2016 12:02 pm I absolutely will welcome this, bring on the Anima anime series!!!

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Joy Evangelion » Sat Dec 10, 2016 7:02 pm Ray wrote: Especially considering the obstacles the last rebuild movie has to surmount in order to be a worthy conclusion to Shinji's story.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by 1731298478 » Tue Feb 07, 2017 5:33 pm Reichu wrote: @OP, I very distinctly remember hearing something to this effect before, though at this point it's hazy whether it was from an official statement somewhere or just fan speculah... I've been able to find an example of the latter through searches, but none of the former. Not terribly satisfying! Does anyone else feel like they heard about "Eva going Gundam" years ago?

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Blockio » Wed Oct 07, 2020 9:46 am Since the tangent regarding it has kind of taken over the outside evidence thread , I'm necroing this one here, this is the much more appropriate place to discuss it. Not to overstep my bounds, but I'd appreciate it if someone with the power to do so would split off the tangent and merge it into here

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Derantor » Sat Oct 10, 2020 10:16 pm I would actually enjoy a Gundam-like broadening of the franchise. It opens up the possibility to have a broader range of stories, to cater to different tastes. Gundam itself offers a wide range of tones and themes as well, so it could be possible for waffy Eva (continuing where the action arc left off, but shifting for the better) to exist alongside the really dark stuff (more in lines with the tone of the descent arc/EoE). I certainly would enjoy animated fanfiction like that.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Blockio » Sun Oct 11, 2020 9:18 am It would definitely be cool to see it happen, yeah. Although at the same time I would wish for these things to take genuinely new turns instead of just remixing the existing story or placing the characters in an arbitrary new setting. The thing I would like to see the most I think is a side story of sorts that tells the events from the perspective of a random civilian, similar to how War in the Pocket does it, but unlike 0080 only from the civilian perspective, since Nerv's side is obviously already known.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Sun Oct 11, 2020 11:24 am I guess I’m the one weirdo here who would like to see Thrice Upon A Time be the true “End of All Evangelion’. No spin-offs. No prequels. No legacy sequels where the next generation takes over. I’d just like to see Evangelion end. Forever. A story can end. And not everything needs an expanded universe. There’s so many good works of entertainment being made that there’s no need to continually dilute one thing because of the familiar name attached to it. Does anyone care for the nth Tenchi Mayo spin-off? Does anyone read the 30th Dune Prequel novel because they really want to? Shrug. I’m in the minority here but I hope the artists at Khara are allowed to do their own things from now on and not have to reverse engineer any future projects they want to do into being Evangelion related. Let it end. I’d welcome a series truly ending for once.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Justacrazyguy » Mon Oct 12, 2020 7:23 am I'm going to second Gendo's Papa here. Not only because I dread the thought of mediocre Evangelion content being made for the next decades, but also because I don't see Eva being nearly as flexible as the Gundam franchise. Admittedly, back in the day Char and Amuro were essential parts of the Gundam universe, but following works showed that all they really needed was the Gundams and a war going on to make it work. The "mechs" are so intrinsically linked to Shinji and the rest of the cast I don't see any alternate universe thing working, and having side stories of characters reacting to the events of te main series also seems a bada idea. I'm being kind of negative. New Eva content made by anyone that isn't Hideaki could be good, but it looks so unlikely to me I'd rather it not happen at all. Not like it's my choice anyway.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by Derantor » Mon Oct 12, 2020 10:26 am As of NTE, the link between Pilot and Eva was radically weakened; the mother soul is not even required anymore, units are freely interchangeable between pilots. And nothing stands in the way of new characters getting their own Evas. There's plenty of potential for stories here. As for the "Evangelion should just end": It already did with EoE! NTE is already more or less fan-fiction. ;P What I'm trying to say is: just like with Star Trek, which went on to create mediocre (or rather: abysmal) movies and series post 2009, nothing is stopping you from rejecting part of this hypothetical Eva-Franchise from your headcanon. It's also a little strange for me to think that Eva could end. While I did say that it did so with EoE, it really didn't. Eva is a story that repeats and that does not give you closure. I doubt that Shin will be any more "final" in its ending than EoTV or EoE. Eva is about the journey, more so than most other stories. And, if nothing else, shitty sequels will spawn delicious fandom-splits and hilarious amounts of angry rants, which can also be highly entertaining.

[]: Re: Anno Khara Expo Interview: Eva Going Gundam; Films/TV/etc. From Different Directors Possible             Quote Post by kuribo-04 » Mon Oct 12, 2020 11:33 am Derantor wrote: As of NTE, the link between Pilot and Eva was radically weakened; the mother soul is not even required anymore, units are freely interchangeable between pilots.
